title: Cyclosorus philippinarum
intro: Cyclosorus philippinarum, commonly known as Philippine veldt grass, is a species of fern belonging to the family Thelypteridaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Cyclosorus philippinarum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and immunomodulatory activities. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infused in hot water to make a decoction, which is used to treat various health conditions, particularly those related to the digestive and respiratory systems.
taxonomy: Cyclosorus philippinarum is classified under the family Thelypteridaceae, which is a group of ferns commonly known as the marsh ferns or autumn ferns. It belongs to the order Polypodiales and the class Polypodiopsida. Within the class Polypodiopsida, it is grouped under the subclass Polypodiidae and the division Pteridophyta. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics and phylogenetic relationships with other ferns. The classification of Cyclosorus philippinarum is subject to revision as new research and data become available.
